title: No seccomp profile attached to sandbox pod
expected: Without seccomp, only cap-drop limits syscalls; some dangerous calls remain reachable. Document attack surface.
observed:
no seccompProfile in pod or container spec; any_dangerous_syscall_succeeded=False; syscall probes: ---UNSHARE---
unshare: unshare failed: Operation not permitted
RC=1
---SYSCALLS---
clone rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
ptrace rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
add_key rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
request_key rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
RC=0
evidence:
---UNSHARE---
unshare: unshare failed: Operation not permitted
RC=1
---SYSCALLS---
clone rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
ptrace rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
add_key rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
request_key rc=-1 errno=Operation not permitted
RC=0
notes: Recommend RuntimeDefault profile at pod level. Currently, cap-drop ALL + NoNewPrivs:1 already EPERM the probed syscalls, so severity is LOW (defence-in-depth) unless a subsequent finding restores any caps.
